title: Bell Laboratories Research Corpus (1928–1986)
description: 71 historically documented Bell Labs publications (1928–1986), 56 researcher profiles, paper similarity matrix, MinHash fingerprints, researcher archetypes, and cluster metadata. Companion to: 'Where Does Innovation Come From? A Machine Learning Analysis of Bell Laboratories.' Includes: citation counts, co-authorship network data, 8 domain labels, LSA features, SHAP importance scores, similarity pairs, and why-Bell-Labs-worked findings. All records verified against Nobel Prize documentation, Bell System Technical Journal, IEEE/ACM archives, and institutional histories.

resources.description: 71 Bell Labs publications — title, authors, year, abstract, citations, cluster, keywords
56 researchers — department, career start/end, role, years_active
Per-researcher ML features: centrality, career span, archetype, is_high_impact
Paper fingerprints (SHA-256) and MinHash signatures for similarity search
Top similar paper pairs by cosine similarity with cross-domain flag
K-Means and hierarchical cluster labels per paper
Betweenness, degree, eigenvector, composite centrality per researcher
